---
title: "Spark SQL Job Data Analysis"
author: "Maria Taylor"
format: html
embed-resources: true
date: "2026-06-02"
date-format: long
execute:
  echo: true
---

```{python}
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession

# Start a Spark session
spark = SparkSession.builder.appName("JobPostingsAnalysis").getOrCreate()

# Load the CSV file into a Spark DataFrame
df = spark.read.option("header", "true").option("inferSchema", "true").option("multiLine", "true").option("escape", "\"").csv("./data/lightcast_job_postings.csv")

# Register the DataFrame as a temporary SQL table
df.createOrReplaceTempView("jobs")

# Display the first five rows
df.show(5)

# Show the schema
df.printSchema()
```
## Query 1: Total Job Postings

```{python}
total = spark.sql("SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_jobs FROM jobs")
total.show()
```

## Query 2: Top 5 Most Common Job Titles

```{python}
top_titles = spark.sql("""
    SELECT TITLE_NAME, COUNT(*) AS job_count
    FROM jobs
    GROUP BY TITLE_NAME
    ORDER BY job_count DESC
    LIMIT 5
""")
top_titles.show()
```

## Query 3: Average Salary by Employment Type

```{python}
avg_salary = spark.sql("""
    SELECT EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME, AVG(SALARY) AS avg_salary
    FROM jobs
    GROUP BY EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME
    ORDER BY avg_salary DESC
""")
avg_salary.show()
```

## Query 4: Top 5 States with Most Job Postings

```{python}
top_states = spark.sql("""
    SELECT STATE_NAME, COUNT(*) AS job_count
    FROM jobs
    GROUP BY STATE_NAME
    ORDER BY job_count DESC
    LIMIT 5
""")
top_states.show()
```

## Query 5: Salary Range for Each Job Title in California

```{python}
ca_salary = spark.sql("""
    SELECT TITLE_NAME, MAX(SALARY) - MIN(SALARY) AS salary_range
    FROM jobs
    WHERE STATE_NAME = 'California'
    GROUP BY TITLE_NAME
    ORDER BY salary_range DESC
    LIMIT 10
""")
ca_salary.show()
```

## Query 6: Top 5 Industries with Highest Average Salaries

```{python}
top_industries = spark.sql("""
    SELECT NAICS2_NAME, AVG(SALARY) AS avg_salary, COUNT(*) AS job_count
    FROM jobs
    GROUP BY NAICS2_NAME
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 100
    ORDER BY avg_salary DESC
    LIMIT 5
""")
top_industries.show()
```
